name: Illinois Currency Exchange
description_short: Loop Currency Exchange offers check cashing, money transfers, vehicle registration, and bill payment services at a Chicago Loop location with extended hours and same-day cash access.
description_long:
Loop Currency Exchange, LLC is a currency exchange and financial services provider located at 214 W. Washington St. in Chicago's Loop district. The business operates as part of the broader Currency Exchange Association network and focuses on serving consumers who need quick access to financial services outside traditional banking hours.

The company's primary service is check cashing, accepting payroll checks, government checks, business checks, tax refunds, unemployment benefits, insurance claims, personal checks, and more without holds. Beyond check cashing, Loop Currency Exchange provides money orders, money transfers, electronic bill pay, and debit card unloading. They also handle vehicle-related services including auto title transfers, license plate renewals, new vehicle registration, and city sticker acquisition. Additional services include notary public, foreign currency exchange, Ventra card sales, postage stamps, photocopies, and rolled coin/cash exchange.

Loop distinguishes itself through its all-in-one service model and stated commitment to personalized customer care rather than corporate institutional banking. The company emphasizes rapid service—cashing checks with verification in minutes—and convenience through multiple location availability. They operate Monday through Friday from 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM at the Loop location and accept both English and Spanish-speaking customers.

As a check-cashing service, Loop Currency Exchange serves an important niche for unbanked and underbanked consumers who need immediate cash access and government service facilitation. However, the company does not disclose check cashing fees on their website, and consumers should expect to pay per-check charges typical of the industry. The service model requires customers to physically visit a location during business hours, and the advertised hours (closed weekends and after 5 PM weekdays) may limit accessibility for some workers.
pros: No holds on cashed checks—immediate access to funds upon verification
Accepts all major check types including payroll, government, business, tax refunds, and personal checks
One-stop shop for financial and government services including vehicle registration, bill pay, and money transfers
Bilingual service available in English and Spanish
Vehicle title and registration services completed with real-time state data entry
Multiple check cashing locations operate extended hours and some 24/7 availability
Fast transaction process requiring only check and valid government ID
cons: No fee schedule disclosed on website—check cashing fees are not transparent
Limited hours at Loop location (closed weekends and after 5 PM weekdays)
Requires in-person visit; no online or mobile check deposit options
Vehicle registration services require specific documentation that varies by transaction type
No indication of competitive fee rates compared to banks or other check cashers
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked workers who need immediate cash access without bank holds
Chicago residents needing vehicle registration, title transfer, or license plate renewal services
Spanish-speaking consumers seeking bilingual financial services
People who need multiple financial transactions handled at a single location
diagnosis: Loop Currency Exchange is best for unbanked and underbanked Chicago residents who need immediate check cashing without bank holds and want to handle vehicle registration in the same visit. The main caveat is that check cashing fees are not disclosed online, so consumers should call ahead to confirm costs before cashing checks, and the limited hours (closed weekends and evenings) may not suit all work schedules.
services: Check cashing (all types including payroll, government, business, personal)
License plate renewals and city stickers
